chapter_context.summary: The Queen of Sheba visits Solomon to test him with hard questions and is awed by his wisdom and wealth; a detailed description of Solomon's golden shields, ivory throne, and trade empire.

outline.text: The Queen of Sheba Visits Solomon to Test His Wisdom
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Queen of Sheba Visits Solomon to Test His Wisdom (10:1-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Solomon's Enormous Wealth; Golden Shields and Ivory Throne
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's Enormous Wealth; Golden Shields and Ivory Throne (10:14-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Solomon's International Trade in Chariots and Horses
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's International Trade in Chariots and Horses (10:23-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 10, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
